svg.title: GitHub Learning Roadmap for Data Scientists and ML Engineers
svg.desc: A five-stage roadmap from Git basics through advanced ML workflows on GitHub

g.onclick: sendPrompt('Teach me Git basics: installing Git, init, add, commit, status, log commands with examples for a data scientist')
g.text: Install Git · git init · add · commit · status · log · .gitignore for datasets

g.onclick: sendPrompt('Teach me GitHub core concepts: creating a repo, clone, push, pull, branches, pull requests — with data science project examples')
g.text: Create repos · clone · push · pull · branches · merge

g.onclick: sendPrompt('Explain GitHub pull requests in detail with a data science workflow example')
sendPrompt('What is a README.md and how should a data scientist write one for their ML project?')
sendPrompt('Explain GitHub Issues and how to use them to track data science tasks and experiments')
sendPrompt('What is the best GitHub workflow for data science projects? Cover notebooks, data versioning, experiment tracking')
g.text: Notebooks on GitHub · branching strategies · managing large files

g.onclick: sendPrompt('How do I use Git LFS or DVC to version large datasets and model files on GitHub?')
sendPrompt('How do I manage Jupyter notebooks cleanly on GitHub? Cover nbstripout, nbconvert, and best practices')
sendPrompt('What is a good branching strategy for ML projects — feature branches, experiment branches, release branches?')
sendPrompt('Explain GitHub Actions for data science: how to automate ML training, testing, and model evaluation using CI/CD pipelines')
g.text: GitHub Actions · auto-test pipelines · model evaluation on every push

g.onclick: sendPrompt('How do I write a GitHub Actions workflow YAML file to run pytest on a machine learning project?')
sendPrompt('How do I deploy a machine learning model using GitHub Actions and Docker?')
sendPrompt('What are GitHub Secrets and how do I use them to store API keys and credentials for ML pipelines?')
sendPrompt('How do I collaborate on ML projects on GitHub at a professional level? Cover forks, contributing guidelines, code review, GitHub Projects')
g.text: Forks · open-source contributions · GitHub profile · showcase ML projects

g.onclick: sendPrompt('How do I fork an open-source ML repo like scikit-learn or HuggingFace and contribute to it?')
sendPrompt('How do I build a strong GitHub profile as a data scientist or ML engineer to impress employers?')
sendPrompt('How do I host a data science project as a GitHub Pages site or Streamlit app linked from GitHub?')
sendPrompt('What are advanced GitHub features for ML engineers? Cover GitHub Codespaces, GitHub Copilot, GitHub Models, and MLOps integrations')
g.text: GitHub Copilot · Codespaces · GitHub Models · MLOps integrations

svg.text: Tap any stage to go deeper
Each card launches a full lesson — follow the roadmap top to bottom
